items.title: fix(build): detect Bun runtime in build scripts
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary

`scripts/build.js`, `scripts/build_package.js`, and the husky pre-commit hook all invoke `npm` and `node` unconditionally, which breaks `bun install` and `bun run build` on systems where bun is the only package manager on PATH. The `prepare` script chain runs `scripts/build.js`, and the pre-commit hook runs on every commit, so this surfaces immediately when trying to set up the repo under Bun.

This branch teaches the build pipeline to detect the runtime and use the equivalent Bun co...

items.title: fix(core): make shell tool work under Bun
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary

Two related runtime issues prevented `bun`-launched builds of gemini-cli from running shell tool calls. The first failure visible to a user is an `ioctl(2) failed, EBADF` crash inside a React `useEffect`. After patching that, the shell tool stops crashing but every command returns empty output. Both stem from `@lydell/node-pty` not behaving correctly when loaded by Bun.

- **No output under Bun.** `getPty()` now short-circuits to the existing `child_process` fallback when `process.ve...

items.title: feat: implicitly use current repo and branch context for pr commands
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview: This PR updates the `gt pr` commands to implicitly infer the repository context from the local git remote if the `--repo` flag is not explicitly provided. This brings `gt` up to parity with standard `gh` behavior. It also modifies `gt pr view` to implicitly view the PR associated with the current checked-out branch when zero arguments are provided.

items.title: fix(editor): typing/pasting ( now actually inserts the character
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary

Two compounding bugs made it impossible to enter an opening paren:

1. **`_auto_insert_pair` discarded its work.** It used Pony stdlib's `String.insert`, which clones and returns a new string rather than mutating in place. The return value was ignored, so the open/close bytes never landed in the buffer — but the cursor advanced anyway, so it looked like \"the keystroke went somewhere.\" The same misuse affected three other sites (auto-indent on newline, `:s` substitute, Ctrl-A/Ctrl-X...

items.title: feat: mouse click moves cursor; drag enters Visual mode
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary
- Switches SGR mouse mode from \`?1000\` (press only) to \`?1002\` (press, release, motion-while-held) and parses the new event types.
- Left-click moves the cursor to the clicked cell. \`_rx_to_cx\` is the inverse of the existing \`_cx_to_rx\` so clicks inside a tab's render-cells land on the tab character, and the gutter snaps to col 0.
- The first drag motion that actually moves the cursor enters Visual mode anchored at the press point; further drag extends the selection. Click in ...

items.title: feat(cli): show acknowledgment when user steering hint is processed
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
Refs #26485

## Summary

When a user submits a steering hint mid-turn, today the CLI silently
splices the hint into the conversation and the user gets no visible
feedback that the hint registered until the model itself acknowledges
it — which can be slow, may be omitted, or per #21508 may even leak
the internal steering instruction back to the user.

This PR adds an immediate, fast acknowledgment line rendered in the
transcript right after the hint is submitted (e.g. `· Understood.
Focusing on ....

items.title: fix(build): detect Bun runtime in build scripts to avoid hardcoded npm
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary

`scripts/build.js`, `scripts/build_package.js`, and the husky `pre-commit` hook all invoke `npm` unconditionally. This breaks `bun install` and committing on Bun-only systems (where `npm` is not on `PATH`), because the `prepare` script chain runs `scripts/build.js` and the pre-commit hook fires on every commit.

Detect the runtime so the equivalent `bun` command is used when `bun` is available and `npm` is not. The `npm` code paths are unchanged for `npm` users.

## Changes

- **`scr...

items.title: Parallelize chunk generation across rayon worker pool
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary
`Chunk::generate()` previously ran on the render thread, throttled to 8 chunks/frame in normal mode and 64 in initial loading. With `VIEW_DISTANCE=35` that's 5,041 chunks sampled serially on a single core while the rest of the CPU sat idle. This PR moves generation onto the existing rayon pool the same way meshing already was, removes per-frame dispatch caps that were tuned for low-core-count machines, and tightens the loading completion gate.

## Changes
- New `Chunk::generation_in_p...

items.title: fix: prevent status bar from wrapping when narrower than its content
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary
- When `left + right` is wider than `_cols` in `_draw_status_bar`, the bar was emitted in full and relied on the terminal to wrap onto a second row.
- That wrap scrolled the screen up by one line, leaving the cursor positioned on the `~` row below the intended buffer line — the distortion seen after a terminal resize.
- Fix: fit-or-truncate the status bar to exactly `_cols` cells (drop the right side first, then truncate the left if needed) so it always occupies a single terminal row....

items.title: fix: ensure closing fence in WrapCodeBlock is on its own line
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary

`WrapCodeBlock` glued the closing ``` ``` ``` directly onto the last line of the content. When the input didn't end with a newline (e.g. a source file without a trailing newline), Markdown renderers never saw the closing fence on its own line and the code block stayed open through the rest of the document.

This appends a newline to the content only when one isn't already present, so inputs that already terminate with `\n` don't gain a spurious blank line before the fence.

Affects b...

items.title: chore(deps): replace image and rayon with smaller alternatives
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary
- Remove `image` crate; replace with direct `png` decoding
- Remove `rayon` crate; replace `rayon::spawn` with `std::thread::spawn`
- Drop `rayon-core`, `crossbeam-*`, `either`, `image`, `num-traits`, and other transitive deps

## Verification
```
cargo check
```
Passes cleanly.

items.title: feat(substitute): add regex support to :s/ command
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary
- Adds a pure-Pony backtracking regex engine (`src/regex.pony`, ~230 LOC)
- Wires it into `:s/`, `:%s/`, and `:.,\$s/` — previously literal-only

## Supported syntax
- Metachars: `.` `^` `$` `*` `+` `?`
- Classes: `[abc]`, `[a-z]`, `[^...]`
- Escapes: `\d \D \w \W \s \S \n \t \r \\` + `\` before any metachar
- Replacement: `&` (whole match), `\&`, `\n`, `\t`, `\r`, `\\`

## Not supported
Capture groups, alternation `|`, lazy quantifiers, `{n,m}`, lookaround.

## Test plan
- [x] `make`...

items.title: feat(runtime): pure-Rust runtime crate; port point.c
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary

- Introduces `crates/runtime/` — a pure-Rust `staticlib` crate that will progressively replace the C runtime under `lib/src/` while preserving the tree-sitter C ABI.
- Ports `lib/src/point.c` as the first module. `ts_point_edit` is implemented in Rust and exported with `extern "C"` linkage.
- Wires the Rust staticlib into the existing CMake build: `point.c` is excluded from the C source glob, `cargo build --release -p tree-sitter-runtime` is invoked via `add_custom_command`, and the ...

items.title: feat(bindings-cpp-ffi): add Rust FFI crate for WASM modules
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary

Add `crates/bindings-cpp-ffi`, a new Rust crate providing type registration and FFI
dispatch for SpacetimeDB WASM modules. It re-implements the logic from
`crates/bindings-cpp` (specifically `module_type_registration.cpp`,
`module_exports.cpp`, and `Module.cpp`) in Rust.

Motivation: The existing C++ bindings have no internal unit tests — all
existing tests are integration-level (WASM compile → publish to server). This
makes it impossible to test type registration edge cases, error p...

items.title: feat: add bun runtime support
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## TLDR

Add support for running Qwen Code with **Bun** runtime for significantly improved performance. Bun provides faster startup times, lower memory usage, and native TypeScript support compared to Node.js.

## Dive Deeper

### Why Bun?

Bun is a modern JavaScript runtime that offers:
- **3-5x faster startup** compared to Node.js for CLI tools
- **Lower memory footprint** - important for long-running sessions
- **Native TypeScript support** - no additional transpilation needed
- **Drop-in com...

items.title: fix: swallow resize() errors after PTY exit on Windows and Unix
items.author: euxaristia
items.body_preview:
## Summary

- Silently ignore `resize()` calls after the PTY process has already exited instead of throwing `'Cannot resize a pty that has already exited'` (Windows)
- Catch `EBADF` errors on `resize()` when the PTY file descriptor is already closed and silently return instead of crashing (Unix)
- Prevents crashes on both platforms when a resize is triggered after process exit (e.g. during terminal cleanup)

Fixes #827

## Details

**Windows:** The native conpty `_exitCode` guard previously thre...
